MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-2006), appearing for defendant / moving party The Olympic Glove & Safety Company, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Joseph Cain

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE OLYMPIC GLOVE & SAFETY COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Olympic Glove & Safety Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the asbestos content issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the gloves and/or blankets at issue contained asbestos. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Olympic gloves and blankets. If the Court were to get to plaintiff's evidence, defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay would be overruled and its objections to plaintiff's Exhibit D would be sustained. As to the issue of whether plaintiff's exposure increased his risk of contracting any asbestos-related disease, defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Mr. Dotson's declaration is insufficient to demonstrate that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. (Rutherford v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 953.) However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Dr. Cohen's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was above ambient air levels, thus disputing the opinions of Mr. Dotson, and also whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
